title: Inevitability and infinite benefit the morality of Seele
display_title: Inevitability and infinite benefit the morality of Seele
wikitext:
[]: Inevitability and infinite benefit... the morality of Seele             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Sun Aug 23, 2015 9:26 pm split from HERE . Reichu wrote: I said that, didn't I? ;p Well, if you're working for a program that needs to use kids to pilot cyborgs to prevent humanity as we know it from being eradicated, it makes perfect sense not to get too attached. What's best for the kids is for them to not be piloting Evas, plain and simple. But if they don't pilot Evas, then they and everyone else will die, and that's not good for anyone. Ergo, the right thing to do is to sacrifice a few kids' safety and happiness so that the entire species can have a future. It screws with one's moral compass, yeah, but you either deal with it, or you lie back and wait for the apocalypse, with the oh-so-comforting knowledge that you could have done something to prevent it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 23, 2015 9:33 pm Sure, but then Instrumentality happens and none of it matters anymore. They were taking the long view of things.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Sun Aug 23, 2015 9:56 pm Bagheera wrote: Sure, but then Instrumentality happens and none of it matters anymore. They were taking the long view of things.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Aug 23, 2015 10:17 pm LordThaeon wrote: Neglecting the important smaller details as well and just how many times were SEELE/Gendo caught with their pants down? Plus, this isn't helping their defense in any ethical or moral sense.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Sun Aug 23, 2015 10:47 pm Bagheera wrote: Morality kinda goes sideways when you're talking about changing the fundamental nature of the human condition, though. If Seele had succeeded no one would care about how the Children were treated, including the Children themselves!

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Sun Aug 23, 2015 11:32 pm I think the whole Morality debate can be summed up with what Maya Ibuki of ALL people says here: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Ash1969 » Sun Aug 23, 2015 11:59 pm xanderkh wrote: I think the whole Morality debate can be summed up with what Maya Ibuki of ALL people says here: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 24, 2015 12:11 am xanderkh wrote: Ask yourself this, even for the greater good, would you rather die as a Human Being, or live the rest of your life as a monster for the so-called "greater good"?

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Mon Aug 24, 2015 12:29 am Reichu wrote: False dichotomy. It's a lot more complicated than this.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 24, 2015 1:44 am xanderkh wrote: In this case, it really isn't, as the whole "greater good" was a lie all along for the selfish "betterment" of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Aug 24, 2015 4:10 pm It's entirely possible that, since these are not their natural bodies, the souls within the Evas simply do not have the strength to activate on their own except under situations of extreme duress. Some of that might be due to the armor, but that seems a bit irrational to me; after all, it would surely be easier to design the stuff such that it suppressed the Eva on demand, rather than all the time. That would eliminate the need for a pilot while still keeping the Eva under control. The fact that they didn't go this route suggests to me that they couldn't, and turned to synchronization out of desperation rather than design. Regarding Instrumentality, y'all are kinda missing the point. Seele's goal was to create a perfect being. Treating people terribly along the way is irrelevant if they're going to ultimately be gifted with transcendent perfection. They won't necessarily want that if asked, but that's a separate issue. In their view any pains or injuries people suffer now won't matter in the end because every human being on Earth, alive and dead, will be part of an eternal, transcendental joy that surpasses all modern human comprehension. They will be getting the ultimate gift in the end, so they will no longer be concerned with their current and past injuries. In other words, Seele are operating using a different set of priorities than we are. Calling them wrong based on our worldview is like calling a Christian wrong for being a bad Buddhist. He's not trying to be a good Buddhist, he's trying to be a good Christian, so his behavior should be viewed in that context. If you want to reject Christianity out of hand and claim Buddhism is the one true path you can of course do that, but that is not a critique of Christianity per se so much as the rejection of a worldview that is not your own simply because it is not your own. Such an approach doesn't allow the two worldviews to meaningfully interact. Now, it is meaningful to say "Seele is forcing us down the path of Christianity without our consent!" and that's true, but their response would be something like "yes, but Heaven's waiting for you at the end, and you'll love it there. And we know this because reasons!" At which point we're left with two options: either they're full of shit, in which case they're madmen, or they're not, in which case their actions become a lot more morally ambiguous. Given EoE I think it's obvious option A is the more accurate case, but I think it's entirely possible they were true believers until the very end and didn't realize just how much they were full of shit.

[]:             Quote Post by LordThaeon » Mon Aug 24, 2015 5:26 pm Please don't quote entire posts. -NemZ And again, it doesn't justify them. Having differing beliefs and perspectives doesn't excuse you from culpability in your actions. And remember that SEELE evidently indirectly awoke/created the angels through engineering Second Impact. Their viewpoint's already invalidated since they've already risked humanity's outright extinction multiple times for the sake of their evolution. And SEELE may not even give a damn about humanity at all if the CI is to be believed. Specifically the part about humanity ceasing to exist entirely once SEELE evolves themselves into godlike beings. And considering that Instrumentality is the thematic representation of "Running Away from life", how is SEELE not full of shit?

[]:             Quote Post by SawItAtAge10 » Tue Aug 25, 2015 6:50 pm Seriously people? Stop it with the massive quotes. -NemZ There in lies the problem with extremism and having an ultimate allegiance to any one thing above human priorities. Such was the problem with Nazi Germany and the "final solution" (all though its true nature was a straight up lie to the non-military personnel) but the the true believers, the general public that went along with it gave into their fanaticism. Such fanaticism is viewed in a different way at the hands of self-righteous zealots. Either people are lying and just do whatever they can to maintain their position of power over others OR (and this is the more horrifying of the two) the people doing harm wholeheartedly believe what they are saying and honestly believe that their actions, even when involves bringing harm to others, is actually good for themselves and the people they are hurting. I've experienced this first hand from Christians who have caused real injury in the name of "saving my soul" not realizing the irony that their actions have only ostracized me further and only intensified my rejection of their religion. But also, the whole context of being a "good Christian" doesn't make very much sense...One can objectively dismiss something and it STILL be an adequate judgement on that particular thing. For example, Nazis. should they be judged exclusively on how good ro bad of a Nazi they were????

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Aug 26, 2015 12:56 am LordThaeon wrote: It would be the equivalent of the first prototype Matrix which failed because no one could accept the false reality created for them: http://matrix.wikia.com/wiki/Paradise_Matrix.

[]: Two can be as bad as one... BP             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Wed Aug 26, 2015 4:29 am Reichu wrote: I thought you were speaking in general terms, so replied accordingly. Personally, I'm mighty suspicious of the need for pilots at all. We see that the Seeds and Angels function perfectly well with just one soul,

[]: Re: Two can be as bad as one... BP             Quote Post by Arcadia's legacy » Wed Aug 26, 2015 4:43 am Alaska Slim wrote: Weaponising the maternal instinct

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 27, 2015 9:00 am LordThaeon wrote: And again, it doesn't justify them.

[]: We're left short of where we should be. Bl             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu Aug 27, 2015 10:38 am Bagheera wrote: The mistake you're making here is this: the Nazis were full of shit.

[]: Re: We're left short of where we should be. Bl             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Aug 27, 2015 11:05 am Alaska Slim wrote: So was SEELE.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Aug 27, 2015 11:14 am And how do you know Seele's ultimate goal isn't the fruition of the FAR's plan? It's impossible to know what the FAR intended for this particular branch of Humanity. Hell, we don't know that they intended anything! For all we know they might have just been hedging their bets and hoping that something outlasted them, at which point Seele's ascended being is as good as anything.

le's goal was to create a perfect being. Treating people terribly along the way is irrelevant if they're going to ultimately be gifted with transcendent perfection. They won't necessarily want that if asked, but that's a separate issue. In their view any pains or injuries people suffer now won't matter in the end because every human being on Earth, alive and dead, will be part of an eternal, transcendental joy that surpasses all modern human comprehension. They will be getting the ultimate gift in the end, so they will no longer be concerned with their current and past injuries. In other words, Seele are operating using a different set of priorities than we are. Calling them wrong based on our worldview is like calling a Christian wrong for being a bad Buddhist. He's not trying to be a good Buddhist, he's trying to be a good Christian, so his behavior should be viewed in that context. If you want to reject Christianity out of hand and claim Buddhism is the one true path you can of course do that, but that is not a critique of Christianity per se so much as the rejection of a worldview that is not your own simply because it is not your own. Such an approach doesn't allow the two worldviews to meaningfully interact. And we know this because reasons!" At which point we're left with two options: either they're full of shit, in which case they're madmen, or they're not, in which case their actions become a lot more morally ambiguous. Given EoE I think it's obvious option A is the more accurate case, but I think it's entirely possible they were true believers until the very end and didn't realize just how much they were full of shit.
